title: uuf2list
text: uuf2list Copy list data from a basic parameter or entity field. Explain this code long uuf2list (UHACT hOpr, int seqNr, UHACT hList); Copy Parameters hOpr The handle to the operation or entity parameter seqNr The sequence number of the basic parameter or entity field (sequence numbers start with 1) hList The handle to a list that will be populated with data. The list must be created with ulistnew . Return Values For more information, see the "Call-In API Return Codes" topic. Use Library: ucall Description uuf2list copies list data from a basic parameter or entity field to the specified list. This function is unconventional because it does not return its output (the list) by reference. Instead, you must first create the list yourself by calling ulistnew , and pass its handle (by value) to uuf2list in the parameter hList . After use, the list should be freed by calling ufreeh . Example: ulistget and uuf2list The following example shows how to retrieve and process a list that was returned as an OUT parameter by a 4GL operation. The operation GETLIST in service TESTSRV is defined as follows: Explain this code operation getlist params string thelist : out; endparams putitem thelist, -1, "One" putitem thelist, -1, "Two" putitem thelist, -1, "Three" end Copy The following 3GL code activates service GETLIST, retrieves the indexed list, and loops through the items to print them out. Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> #include <stdio.h> void get_itemlist () { UHACT hEnv, hIn; /* Environment and instance handle */ UHACT hOp; /* Operation handles for OP1 amd OP2 */ long actStatus, procErr; /* Activation status and procerr */ UHACT hList; /* Item list handle */ int i; /* Loop variable */ UCHAR *pItem; ueopen(&hEnv); /* Get environment handle */ /* Instantiate service TESTSVC */ uinstnew (hEnv, (UCHAR *)"TESTSVC", 0, 0, UDEFAULT_COMM_MODE, 0, &hIn, &procErr); /* Instantiate operation GETLIST */ uinstopr (hIn, (UCHAR *)"GETLIST", 0, &hOp); uopract(hOp, &actStatus, &procErr); /* Activate the operation */ ulistnew(hEnv, &hList); /* Create an item list */ uuf2list(hOp, 1, hList); /* Get the list data */ i=1; /* Loop through list items */ while ( ulistget(hList, UITEM_OPTION_NONE, i, 0, &pItem) == UACT_SUCCESS ) { printf("Item %d = '%s'\n", i, pItem); /* Print out item */ unifree(hList, pItem); /* Free item data */ i++; } uinstdel (hIn, 0); /* Destroy instance */ ufreeh (&hList); /* Free list handle */ ufreeh (&hOp); /* Free operation handle */ ufreeh (&hIn); /* Free instance handle */ ufreeh (&hEnv); /* Free environment handle */ } Copy This code prints out: Explain this code Item 1 = 'One' Item 2 = 'Two' Item 3 = 'Three' Copy Related Links Call-In API Return Codes Codes that can possibly be returned by functions from the 3GL call-in API are consistent with the values returned by the ProcScript function $procerror, unless noted otherwise. ulist2uf Copy item list data to a basic parameter or entity field. ulistget Get an item from an item list. ulistnew Create an item list.
